name: sigma70_promoter_tuning
description: Bacterial expression cassette with a *variable* sigma70 promoter and a *variable* rho-independent terminator flanking a fixed reporter context. The variable 50 bp promoter is scored by the `sigma70-promoter` constraint against the canonical E. coli consensus (-35 box `TTGACA`, -10 box `TATAAT`, spacer 17 bp; Hawley & McClure 1983), and by `gc-content` and `max-homopolymer` to keep the upstream sequence within a synthesis-friendly composition window. Between the promoter and terminator we hold three pieces fixed because the optimizer cannot enforce frame-integrity at the constraint level: a strong synthetic Shine-Dalgarno + 5' UTR + ATG start codon + NdeI cloning site (5'-AAGGAGGAGAAAATGCATATG-3'; SD core AGGAGG matches the anti-SD region of E. coli 16S rRNA), a fixed 84 bp synthetic codon-optimized prefix encoding the first ~28 residues of sfGFP (Pédelacq et al. 2006, PDB 2B3P) as a representative reporter CDS prefix, and a TAA stop. Replace the SD with the literal Anderson B0034 part (`AAAGAGGAGAAA`) or a Salis RBS Calculator-tuned part (Salis et al. 2009) for canonical E. coli expression; replace the sfGFP prefix with your own CDS to retarget. The 40 bp terminator segment is variable; we drive it toward high GC (55-75%) consistent with the GC-rich stem typical of rho-independent intrinsic terminators (Lesnik et al. 2001, Nudler & Gottesman 2002), with a `max-homopolymer` cap so the U-tract that normally follows the stem isn't degraded into a runaway A/T run during sampling. This is a focused teaching example for two-region tuning around a fixed reporter; for a richer multi-construct multi-segment template see `multiple_segments.json`.

segments.label: Variable sigma70 promoter region (-35 / spacer / -10 / discriminator)
Fixed strong synthetic SD + 5' UTR + ATG + NdeI cloning context (21 bp)
Fixed sfGFP-style CDS prefix, first ~28 residues codon-optimized for E. coli (84 bp)
Variable rho-independent terminator (target: GC-rich stem + biased toward U-rich 3' tract)
